stem: The polynomial \( x^{3} + ([[c2]])x^{2} + ([[c1]])x + ([[c0]]) \) has three roots \( r_1, r_2, r_3 \) (counted with multiplicity). What is the product \( r_1 r_2 r_3 \)?
solution: By Vieta's formulas for a monic cubic \( x^{3} + bx^{2} + cx + d \), the product of the roots is \( -d \). Here \( d = [[c0]] \), so \( r_1 r_2 r_3 = -([[c0]]) = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This is the constant term \( d \) itself; the product of the roots is \( -d \), so the sign is wrong.
This is the sum of the roots \( -b \), not their product.
This is the sum of the pairwise products of the roots, not the full product.
This is off by one from the correct product.
This is off by one from the correct product.
This is off by two from the correct product.

stem: The polynomial \( x^{3} + ([[c2]])x^{2} + ([[c1]])x + ([[c0]]) \) has three roots \( r_1, r_2, r_3 \) (counted with multiplicity). What is the sum \( r_1 + r_2 + r_3 \)?
solution: By Vieta's formulas for a monic cubic \( x^{3} + bx^{2} + cx + d \), the sum of the roots is \( -b \). Here \( b = [[c2]] \), so \( r_1+r_2+r_3 = -([[c2]]) = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This is the coefficient \( b \) itself; the sum of the roots is \( -b \), so the sign is wrong.
This is the product of the roots \( -d \), not their sum.
This is the sum of the pairwise products of the roots, not the sum of the roots themselves.
This is off by one from the correct sum.
This is off by one from the correct sum.
This is off by two from the correct sum.

stem: The polynomial \( x^{3} + ([[c2]])x^{2} + ([[c1]])x + ([[c0]]) \) has three roots \( r_1, r_2, r_3 \) (counted with multiplicity). What is \( r_1 r_2 + r_1 r_3 + r_2 r_3 \)?
solution: By Vieta's formulas for a monic cubic \( x^{3} + bx^{2} + cx + d \), the sum of the pairwise products of the roots equals the coefficient \( c \) directly. Here \( c = [[c1]] \), so \( r_1r_2+r_1r_3+r_2r_3 = [[c1]] = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This negates the coefficient \( c \), but the sum of pairwise products equals \( c \) directly with no sign change.
This is the coefficient \( b \), which gives the negative of the sum of the roots, not the pairwise products.
This is the product of the roots, not the sum of their pairwise products.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by two from the correct value.

stem: The quadratic \( ([[a]])x^{2} + ([[b]])x + ([[c]]) = 0 \) has roots \( r_1 \) and \( r_2 \). What is \( r_1 + r_2 \)?
solution: For \( ax^{2}+bx+c \), Vieta's formulas give \( r_1+r_2 = -\dfrac{b}{a} = -\dfrac{[[b]]}{[[a]]} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This has the correct magnitude but the wrong sign; the sum is \( -b/a \), not \( b/a \).
This is the negative of the PRODUCT of the roots, \( -c/a \), not their sum.
This is the product of the roots \( c/a \), not their sum.
This is off by one from the correct sum.
This is off by one from the correct sum.
This is off by two from the correct sum.

stem: The polynomial \( x^{3} + ([[c2]])x^{2} + ([[c1]])x + ([[c0]]) \) has three roots \( r_1, r_2, r_3 \). Using Vieta's formulas (without finding the roots individually), what is \( r_1^{2} + r_2^{2} + r_3^{2} \)?
distractor_rationales: This forgets to subtract off the pairwise-product term entirely.
This adds the pairwise-product term instead of subtracting it.
This drops the squared sum-of-roots term, keeping only the correction.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by two from the correct value.

stem: The polynomial \( x^{3} + ([[c2]])x^{2} + ([[c1]])x + ([[c0]]) \) has three nonzero roots \( r_1, r_2, r_3 \). What is \( \dfrac{1}{r_1} + \dfrac{1}{r_2} + \dfrac{1}{r_3} \)?
solution: Writing the reciprocal sum over a common denominator, \( \dfrac{1}{r_1}+\dfrac{1}{r_2}+\dfrac{1}{r_3} = \dfrac{r_1r_2+r_1r_3+r_2r_3}{r_1r_2r_3} \). By Vieta's formulas the numerator is \( [[c1]] \) and the denominator is \( -([[c0]]) \), so the reciprocal sum is \( \dfrac{[[c1]]}{-([[c0]])} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This has the correct magnitude but the wrong sign, forgetting that the product of the roots is \( -d \), not \( d \).
This inverts the fraction, using the product over the pairwise-sum instead of the reverse.
This inverts the fraction and also gets the sign wrong.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by two from the correct value.
